data.title: Nomura downgrades Coupang amid fallout from the massive data breach
data.description:
Nomura downgraded Coupang (CPNG) to a Neutral rating on Tuesday after having the e-commerce stock slotted at Buy.

Analyst Angela Hong pointed to a notable investor story shift on Coupang (CPNG) from structural growth to heightened regulatory compliance

"While we initially viewed the 30 November data breach incident as a temporary drag on its share price, the company now faces increased regulatory oversight in Korea," warned Hong. It was noted that the FTC has signaled its intent for rigorous administrative measures, including potential operational adjustments, to ensure higher social responsibility and consumer protection. As a result, Nomura cut its 2026 EPS estimate on Coupang (CPNG) by 95% to $0.03 vs. $0.17 consensus, factoring in the consumer compensation package and a potential $900M fine.

Shares of Coupang (CPNG) slipped 0.5% in premarket trading and are now down more than 14% over the last six weeks as the major data breach story played out.

data.title: nLIGHT expects Q4 revenue between $78M to $80M
data.description:
• nLIGHT (LASR) on Tuesday said that it expects to report fourth quarter 2025  revenue in the range of $78 million to $80 million (vs consensus of $75.19M), above the high-end of the company’s previously announced fourth quarter guidance range of $72 million to $78 million.
  • The company expects to report Laser Products revenue of $54 million to $55 million and Advanced Development revenue of ~$24 million to $25 million.
  • The anticipated upside in revenue is primarily due to the continued strength in it’s revenue from the Aerospace & Defense market, nLIGHT  added.
  • LASR +2.43% premarket to $42.58.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation NON CUM PFD SE K declares $0.3844 dividend
data.description:
• The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation NON CUM PFD SE K (BK.PR.K) declared $0.3844/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Payable March 20; for shareholders of record March 5; ex-div March 5.
  • See BK.PR.K Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: GDS sells $385M worth DayOne shares, plans to reallocate funds to China
data.description:
• GDS (GDS) announced the sale of ordinary shares in DayOne Data Centers Limited valued at $385M. 
  • This transaction involves DayOne repurchasing the shares from GDS (GDS) at the same price as its recent Series C convertible preferred share issuance.
  • DayOne is a Singapore-headquartered hyperscale data center platform where GDS (GDS) holds a minority stake, and the buyback will allow it to recover about 95% of its principal investment at nearly a 6.5 times multiple. 
  • GDS plans reallocate proceeds and invest in compelling new business opportunities with attractive return potential in its core business in China.
  • The value of GDS’s remaining stake implied by the Series C new issue price is over $2.2B, equivalent to $11.18 per GDS (GDS) American Depositary Share.
  • The stock price fell about 3% on Tuesday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: National Vision gains after updating guidance ahead of its ICR appearance
data.description:
National Vision Holdings (EYE) issued a guidance update on Tuesday ahead of the retailer participating later in the day at the ICR Conference in Orlando, Florida.

The optical retailer reaffirmed its fiscal 2025 outlook for the 53-week period that ended on January 3. National Vision (EYE) guided for revenue of $1.970B to $1.988B on adjusted comparable store sales growth of 5.0% to 6.0%. National Vision (EYE) also sees full-year EPS of $0.63 to $0.71 (midpoint $0.67) vs. $0.70 consensus and adjusted operating income of $92M to $98M. The company expects to have opened 32 new stores for the full year.

Shares of National Vision Holdings (EYE) edged 1.4% higher in premarket trading after gaining 4.1% on Monday. The stock is up more than 150% over the last year. Short interest stands at 12.6% of the total float.

data.title: Five Below is downgraded at Jefferies on a valuation call
data.description:
Jefferies downgraded Five Below (FIVE) to a Hold rating from Buy on a call tied to valuation. While the retailer's recent momentum was tied to CEO Winnie Park's leadership and refined merchandising strategy, tougher comparisons in the year ahead are expected to set a high bar. 

Analyst Randal Konik said with Five Below's (FIVE) valuation now well above its three-year average, the firm sees room for multiple compression as comparable sales growth normalizes back toward the long-term algorithm. He highlighted that Five Below (FIVE) has delivered comparable sales marks of 7.1%, 12.4%, and 14.3% through the first three quarters and expects 14.5% in Q4.

"This performance led management to raise its full-year comp outlook to 12.5% compared with the prior 3 to 5% range earlier this year. A meaningful portion of this momentum has been driven by ticket gains, which increased 3.4% in the second quarter and 7.2% in the third quarter, supported by $5-and-up items. As a result, the company faces a tougher setup in F’26 as it will be lapping double-digit, ticket-driven comp growth, while historically results have leaned more on traffic and transactions, making sustained momentum harder to achieve," warned Konik.

Jefferies set a price target of $210 on Five Below (FIVE).

Shares of Five Below (FIVE) were down 1.8% in premarket trading.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: JPMorgan Chase tops Q4 estimates; investment banking revenue misses
Earnings Snapshot: Bank of New York Mellon posts Q4 double-beat
Argenx bid for Vyvgart label expansion undergoes FDA priority review
data.description:
Argenx (ARGX) announced on Tuesday that the U.S. FDA has granted priority review for its marketing application aimed at widening the approvability of Vyvgart, its blockbuster treatment for a rare autoimmune disease known as generalized myasthenia gravis.

The company stated that the regulator has accepted its supplemental Biologics License Application for Vyvgart, targeting adults with acetylcholine receptor antibody (AChR-Ab)- negative generalized myasthenia gravis ((gMG)). The FDA has issued May 10, 2026, as the target action date.

The sBLA is supported by data from Argenx’s (ARGX) Phase 3 ADAPT SERON study, which met the primary endpoint related to a gMG clinical measure over four weeks.

Vyvgart is already available in the U.S. for adults with gMG who are anti-acetylcholine receptor ((AChR)) antibody positive. The neonatal Fc receptor ((FcRn)) blocker generated $2.2B in global product net sales for the Dutch drugmaker in 2024.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Delta Air Lines Q4 revenue beats, FY profit guidance lags estimates
